metadata.title: WattWise 2026 Comprehensive Electrician Licensing Exam Curriculum
metadata.validationStatus: 92 full lessons, 220+ flashcards, 305+ practice questions with difficulty levels, 8th-12th grade reading level
limitations: Content is educational reference material; supplement with hands-on training and actual NEC code.
Exam requirements vary by jurisdiction; use as national foundation with jurisdiction-specific overlays.
Verify current code with official NFPA publications for latest updates.

executiveSummary.overview: WattWise provides a comprehensive, research-backed electrician licensing exam curriculum covering Apprentice (28 lessons), Journeyman (32 lessons), and Master (32 lessons) levels. All 92 lessons use 8th-12th grade reading level with real-world examples and NEC references. The expanded content pack includes 94 flashcards and 305 practice questions with difficulty ratings.
contentApproach: Every lesson explains WHY the rule matters before diving into WHAT the rule says.
Practice questions include difficulty ratings (Easy, Moderate, Difficult) to guide self-assessment.
Flashcards focus on terminology, definitions, and quick-reference content for efficient study.
Real-world scenarios and jobsite examples make abstract rules concrete and memorable.
All content uses simple, accessible language appropriate for working electricians.

fullLessons.lessonTitle: Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law

lessonContent.heading: Learning Objective
lessonContent.body: Master Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law to succeed on your Apprentice exam and apply these concepts safely on the job.

lessonContent.heading: Why This Matters
lessonContent.body: Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law is essential for electrical safety and NEC compliance. Real electricians encounter this concept regularly on the job. Understanding the WHY behind rules helps you remember and apply them correctly in new situations.

lessonContent.heading: Core Concept
lessonContent.body: The fundamental principle behind Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law is that electrical safety depends on proper design, installation, and maintenance. This topic directly connects to multiple NEC articles and practical installation requirements that you will encounter throughout your career.

lessonContent.heading: Practical Example
lessonContent.body: In real-world applications, Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law affects how you design and install electrical systems safely and efficiently. Understanding this concept helps you make better decisions on the job and explains why the NEC has specific rules.

lessonContent.heading: Common Misconceptions
lessonContent.body: Many apprentices and journeymen misunderstand Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law. A common mistake is confusing related concepts or applying the wrong rule. The key is understanding the purpose of the rule, not just memorizing the rule number.

lessonContent.heading: Real-World Application
lessonContent.body: When you encounter Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law on the job, remember that the NEC rules exist to protect people and property. Think about the safety purpose behind each rule, and you will be better equipped to apply it correctly in new situations.

lessonContent.heading: Exam Insights
lessonContent.body: Exam questions about Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law often test your understanding of when and how to apply the rule. They rarely ask you to simply state the rule; instead, they present a scenario and ask you to solve a problem or identify what is wrong.

lessonContent.heading: Key References
lessonContent.body: The primary NEC reference for Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law is listed below. Take time to read the actual code text, not just summaries. Understanding the exact wording helps you answer exam questions correctly.

keyTakeaways: Voltage, Current, Resistance, and Ohm's Law is a fundamental concept in electrical work.
Understanding the purpose of rules helps you apply them in new situations.
This topic connects to multiple NEC articles and practical requirements.
Real electricians must master this concept to work safely and compliant.
